mainEntity.name: What kind of settlement can I expect for a shattered ankle from a work fall in Boise?

acceptedAnswer.text: Settlements are based on your pre-injury wages and the level of permanent physical impairment. You are entitled to medical costs, two-thirds of your lost wages, and a permanent disability payout if the injury is lasting.
acceptedAnswer.description: The value of a slip and fall claim depends on your "Average Weekly Wage" and the "Permanent Partial Disability" (PPD) rating assigned by a doctor. Workers' comp is not like a personal injury lawsuit; you don't get a "jackpot" for pain and suffering. Instead, Idaho law dictates a specific percentage of your wages while you are unable to work (Temporary Total Disability). If your ankle requires surgery or results in a permanent limp, you will receive a lump sum based on the impairment rating. Complex fractures or ligament tears that prevent you from returning to your specific job can significantly increase the case value. An attorney can help ensure the insurance company's doctor doesn't give you an unfairly low disability rating.
